reviews.review_description:
I’ve always been a fan of psychedelic VR experiences, including fractals, but no other app presents them in such uniquely constructed ways, or with such clear visuals.
The way some are manipulated to look like landscapes, the way you can see through semi-clear areas to places further beneath, I’ve never seen a fractal VR experience so developed.
I love it.
If you like it, you might also want to try Visionarium.

If you enjoy watching fractal fly through videos, you'll love this.  The music is great and the visuals will absolutely blow you away with sharpness and detail not seen in most games/apps.   Granted, the 10 videos are huge and playing locally, it doesn't stream them, so the quality is amazing with no stutter.  It takes up quite a bit of space (20+ gigs).  The videos are 3D 180 8k, so you don't need to worry about looking behind, you can just get comfortable and zone out. My only issue was in remembering to blink.  Highly recommended, I hope they expand the app with more videos soon.
Light, colour and movement, beautiful.

By far my favourite VR experience. Other worldly, incredible music and visual experience that will stay with you forever. You will show this to friends and family and they will also remember it forever. Oddly life affirming making you feel both powerful and insignificant at the same time. Astonishing.

It's so unique and unforgettable, a mind-blowing experience.

Filters weaken, memory and familiar shapes lose meaning, and the observer — the “I” — fades. Ego tires, loses its grip, and steps aside, leaving only pure process. There are no characters, stories, or emotions — just high-entropy fractals, a world without an observer, form without explanation.

For many, it’s unsettling, but if you let go, it opens a state of emptiness and awareness without a subject. After this, ordinary art feels noisy, people excessive, narratives artificial.

reviews.review_title: If you lost your UI and can't interact
reviews.review_description:
I launched another app vhile was downloading in Recombination and then, when I came back, there was no UI. Basically what you need to do is fee your RAM on Quest. I did it using Quest game optimazer but I belive you can just restart your quest.

Enother problem with only several vids download: only several vids can be in download at a time, so just be patient and whait previous vids to be downloaded. 

And yes the vids are heavy, so one 2 min vid could be 2,5+ GB. And there are vids 9 min long. Hopefully it  helps, as I do not see any replies from Devs..
Simply mesmerizing!!! I hope they will continue adding more abstract worlds.

This app / game has a problem with the menus on the Meta Quest 3. It does not allow any interaction. No cursors are shown on the screen even after reinstall. There are several other reviews that mention this issue as well.

Trying to download the videos that provide the experiences associated with the app is absolutely awful. Because of this the highest rating for this app I can give is 3 stars. A lesser free alternative use the youtube app and do a search for fractal vr videos. Not the same quality but it's free. The creators of this app need to address the videos download issue with this app. The videos sometimes takes multiple days to download. Note I have fast internet service and the other apps don't suffer from the issue this app has.
Not enough content to justify the price. Even with 40% off

Just wow. 
Hope there's more coming...
U got like 4 hours left on sale. Buy this, listen to algebra of awe (deep) and if you aren’t blown away refund it

reviews.review_title: Can’t do anything in the game
reviews.review_description: I can’t do anything thing in the game, can’t select anything can’t download anything
Now THIS shows what's imaginable with VR. Thank you.

Magnificent


More please

This is truly the best VR experience I've ever had. Can you make it so that you can see the hands? Currently, the controller only appears when pause. If i can see and use my hands while watching a video, I'd love to make content on it. Please

reviews.review_title: I can’t download all the videos
reviews.review_description: It says that my location doesn’t let me download all the videos but only 4 of them… what that’s mean? I really wish maybe which location I need to put on
This is 1 of the best experience i every had in VR. 5*

I loved every one of the options avaliable, i wish there were more. It is incredible. My only complaint is that it is not 360°. And also another complaint, but it is minor, it is that i wish that there were more movement in the fractal themselves like in some parts of the second video.
wonderful atmosphere, I get goosebumps when I admire.

reviews.review_title: Not as cool as it looks
reviews.review_description: Needs a lot more content for how little you get from it saddly. More interaction is needed and more selection. For a vr game it is very lacking in features

reviews.review_title: Worth a try but there seems to be glitch
reviews.review_description: How can I contact the developers? I'm debating whether to request refund. The music is lovely and visuals are interesting. But two issues. My display has rainbow sprinkles that are intrusive. This only occurs in this app. My device was fine before. I saw another review that mentioned there were rain particles on the display. In addition, when I quit the app, the music is still playing. You can't quit the app unless you turn off your device.

I wouldnt buy this for meditation/therapeutic reasons. This is supposed to be VR. 360 DEGREES NOT 180. you cannot look behind you. The videos dont last for a long time. But your bad nerves/stress will. Save your money. If you buy this, you will regret it.

Before discovering Recombination, Tripp and Tetris Effect were my go-to’s for VR visual experiences. But Recombination completely redefines the genre for me—it’s simply on another level of immersion. The detail in every aspect is astounding, and the music complements the visuals perfectly. For the best experience, I highly recommend using external headphones. It’ll take you deeper down the rabbit hole

An amazing and beautiful set of voyages through fractal landscapes, now with two more tracks. Hopefully the author will be able to fund more tracks to be added as bonuses through the life cycle, providing better value for early adopters who have issues with the cost.  
The only downside is the file management and file size. 
1/ Once a track is downloaded there seems to be no way to remove it other than 'Remove all data'.
2/ Make the meaning of 'local download' clearer. Simpy put, 'local' means your headset memory - if it's full you can't download tracks. It does appear that this is a restriction to entitlement or rights issue (see reviews) but it is not, it is a space issue. It would be clearer to say 'Not enough memory'.
3/ Offer "radio edit" downloads of the longer tracks, so that they can be previewed faster and/or hold more tracks for variety.

It is nice, it's a little pricey,  but I enjoy the artists work so I don't mind supporting him. But i wish this app had a fov slider and that we could rotate the view with the thumbsticks. When you pause the video it zooms all the way out.  I kinda wish I could watch the video zoomed out.  I just feel like we are too zoomed in.  Also sometimes the app memory crashes and I get this strange digital rain effect that is distracting.

Also.  I think some of the videos go too long without motion.  The whole set can morph and change colors. So why do I spend whole minutes looking at static geometry.

I bought the app and downloaded all the sessions, but none of them worked. I just have something that looks like a screensaver. I deleted and reloaded the app, too. Nothing. I hope it's working for some people. This is not what I was expecting...
You have to download all of the content and it took all my memory. I’d like a refund please

I tried to download this about a year ago but left negative feedback as it would only let me download 4 of the videos. I got a refund and decided to try downloading it again. This time it has worked and have got all 10 videos downloaded. I have changed my feedback to five stars now. I enjoy the app and love the soothing music that goes with the animation. Well done developers and thank you for spring out the problem I originally had.

developer_response.body: Hi, we're sorry that you're experiencing problems with the app. Let's see what we can do. The videos are very high quality 8k videos that the app downloads locally, but it might be that there isn't enough space on your Quest. What you could consider is make room by removing other apps. Or, if that doesn't work, remove the videos you've already seen, and download the other ones. Happy viewing!

reviews.review_title: Very nice indeed & keeps getting better!
reviews.review_description:
The fractal art is absolutely on point, really beautiful & the music works very well with it, combining certain effects with changes in the music & I really love this. 
The developer has recently added several more really nice experiences for free & if I’m not mistaken, he seems to be constantly fine tuning the graphical settings & resolution & that only makes it look even more beautiful! 
I love these type of experiences, probably more than games. 10/10. 
Lovely stuff!!!

this artistic experience is just magnificent, the only problem I have is that certain scenes are not smooth, it's jerky and need update performance otherwise we can have motion sickness. And please a free DLC because it's a little bit expensive.
But i really love this Amazing experience from another World.

This is a collection of 8 pre-rendered VR180 videos of 3D fractals by fractal artist Julius Horsthuis. Each video is accompanied by an electronic music track, but there is only the most basic of synchronization with the music. The videos range in length from about 3 minutes to 6 minutes. The viewing angle seems to be slightly offset, so you get about 80 degrees in one direction and 100 degrees in the other; this can help give the impression the videos are more than 180 degrees provided you look around in the right direction.

I believe Horsthuis uses the program Mandelbulb 3D to generate these. You can get a good taste by finding similar videos on Youtube, including ones by Horsthuis, but in a lower resolution (4K, while I think this app uses 8K). Use YouTube VR and search for “mandelbulb VR180”. It’s worth doing that to get an idea of what you are getting here.

3D fractals are very trippy. Sometimes they’re almost creepy. In addition, the evolving fractal and camera movement could cause some motion sickness in those prone to it.

While these videos are great, its hard to recommend this app at the price. I think it would have made more sense for the app to be free and have samples of each, and sell the individual videos separately as DLC. That would give people a  better idea of what they are getting and open up the app to those who may be interested but not $40 interested.

Each video is accompanied by an electronic music track, but there is only the most basic of synchronization with the music. The videos range in length from about 3 minutes to 6 minutes. While these videos are great, its hard to recommend this app at the price. I think it would have made more sense for the app to be free and have samples of each, and sell the individual videos separately as DLC. That would give people a  better idea of what they are getting and open up the app to those who may be interested but not $40 interested.
Amazing!  Looking forward to viewing this on Quest 3 :)

I've been waiting for this to drop for awhile.  If you like anything psychedelic, this is for you.  The quality is amazing, very high resolution and fun to go through.  I also own Visionarium, which is very similar.  The main difference are this has higher resolution 8k 180 degree video, while Visionarium is rendered live.   Visionarium has interactivity with your controllers and the music though, which Recombination does not.  Instead  it focuses on the best visual experience.  I'd recommend the developer puts a 2-3 min sample on oculus tv so people can preview it in full quality.  The price is pretty high, but I'm sure a lot of work went into it.  If you want 25% off you can use my link: vr.miketunes.com/recombination

I was blown away, speechless, and a few times even emotional. I hope my heaven has a room like this…

Thank you for taking the time to create this, please  make add ons, or another vr project! Worth any price for the most mind bending experience you can have sitting on a couch!

Recombination is a triumphant audio visual delight! 

The music and visuals merge to create an immersive experience that succeeds where many VR experiences tend to leave you disappointed.

The attention to detail and texture is amazing in VR, it really makes the fractal worlds Julius crafts feel that much more tangible - a favorite with everyone I’ve been sharing it with too!

reviews.review_title: Best app since oculus launch imo
reviews.review_description:
Mesmerizing experience, I wasn't sure it will be worth the money but this piece of art experience is one of the most intense and beautiful I had in my life. 
Words can't do justice. 

One of my favorite driver with VR is to share with others striking experience, this one is definitely going to be my top pick. 
I really hope more video will follow, but I can imagine that's a huge work.

Thanks for sharing tutorial videos on your website explaining how you create those scenes.
It's a movie with no freedom of movement at all. The movie is pretty, with loads of fractals and nice music. It's a bit boring though.

One of the best experiences you can get on the quest store. You have to download the tracks, so you need space on your quest2 to do this. The quality is very good. It deserves 5 stars even if the price is very high.

I have been waiting for an app like this since the boom of VR. The visuals and music are expertly crafted. It is truly a work of full-spectrum sensory art. For anyone looking to supplement their transcendent experiences and meditation, this is a must.
